name: Orca

runner.notes: Install Orca, open the prepared worktree, provide prompt.md exactly, then run harness verification. Later adapter candidate: Orca CLI/server if a stable non-interactive run path is documented.

inclusion: Direct open-source ADE competitor for running multiple coding agents across isolated worktrees.
pricing_model: Open-source app plus bring-your-own agent/model subscriptions.
public_stack_signals: Electron desktop app with TypeScript, React, Vite/electron-vite, node-pty, xterm, ssh2, ws, and mobile-related repo folders.
CLI binary exposed as orca in package metadata; package.json version observed 1.4.142-rc.7 on 2026-07-15.
Remote server mode runs orca serve on a headless machine.
GitHub positioning remains fleet-of-parallel-agents ADE; high visibility (~19.7k stars at refresh).
moat_hypotheses: broad product surface across desktop, mobile, browser, GitHub/Linear, and remote runtime
open-source distribution and strong GitHub visibility
worktree-first mental model for racing and reviewing agent outputs
setup_notes: Start with manual desktop run. For remote parity, use a machine where target agent CLIs and credentials are installed.

sources.notes: README, feature list, license, supported agents, and repository language signals. Refresh 2026-07-15: still MIT TypeScript; pushed same day; ~19.7k stars.
Runtime/package signals: Node 24, Electron, React, Vite, TypeScript, node-pty, xterm, ssh2, ws, pnpm, Monaco, and Tailwind. Refresh 2026-07-15 version field: 1.4.142-rc.7.
Remote Orca Server model and orca serve setup.
Custom CLI agent support and worktree cwd behavior.

research.positioning: Worktree IDE/ADE for fleets of parallel coding agents using existing CLI agents and subscriptions.

data: usage/rate-limit tracking per supported agent where configured
unknowns: Mobile app internal implementation and sync protocol details are not fully mapped in this pass.

infrastructure: Local desktop app owns repos, worktrees, terminals, tabs, provider checks, and agent sessions.
Remote Orca Server lets a headless machine own runtime state while clients connect over a controlled network path.
SSH worktrees support remote execution and port forwarding.

agent_support: Any CLI agent that runs in a terminal
Documented support includes Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor, Copilot, Grok, Auggie, Gemini/Antigravity, Kiro, Qwen, Devin, and others
orchestration: Fan one prompt across multiple worktrees
Quick open across worktrees, files, agents, commands, and repo context
CLI automation commands such as worktree create, snapshot, click, and fill
workspace_isolation: Working directory for CLI agents is the current worktree
SSH and remote server runtime ownership
review_shipping: Hosted reviews, GitHub issues/actions, Linear/Jira drawers
remote_mobile: Mobile companion for monitoring and steering agents

install_surface: Desktop app for macOS, Windows, Linux
parity_risks: Large feature surface may make manual runs faster than products without GUI helpers.
Mobile/remote features are not exercised by the current v0.1 local task suite.
CLI automation path needs separate validation before replacing manual protocol.

hypotheses: Open-source + high visibility creates distribution advantage.
Worktree-first fleet UX is a strong direct competitor to Tachyon's orchestration story.
Mobile and remote server surfaces expand use cases beyond local desktop.
evidence: Official README lists parallel worktrees, mobile companion, terminal splits, design mode, native GitHub/Linear, SSH worktrees, diff annotation, and CLI.
Official docs describe remote server mode and custom CLI agent support.
Refresh 2026-07-15: GitHub remains highly active with large star count; landing still positions Orca as an ADE for agent fleets.
unknowns: Quality of verification gates and evidence capture compared with Tachyon remains unbenchmarked.
